title: Traveller Mainworld
description: A generated mainworld record produced by traveller_world_gen.py, following the Traveller 2022 Core Rulebook Universe Creation rules (pp. 248-261).

name.description: The world's name as provided to the generator.

uwp.description: Universal World Profile string. Format: Starport + Size + Atmosphere + Hydrographics + Population + Government + Law – TechLevel, all in Traveller eHex (0–9, A–Z).

code.description: Starport class. A = Excellent … E = Frontier, X = No Starport.

description.description: Human-readable summary of facilities available at this starport class.

code.description: Size code (0 = asteroid / orbital complex; 8 ≈ Earth; 10 = large world).

diameter_km.description: Approximate equatorial diameter as a human-readable string (e.g. '12,800' or '<1,000').

surface_gravity.description: Approximate surface gravity as a human-readable string (e.g. '1.00G', 'negligible').

code.description: Atmosphere code (0 = None; 6 = Standard/Earth-like; 11 = Corrosive; 16 = Gas, Helium; 17 = Gas, Hydrogen — WBH NHZ codes).

name.description: Descriptive name for the atmosphere type (e.g. 'Standard', 'Insidious').

survival_gear.description: Minimum survival gear required for unprotected exposure (e.g. 'Vacc Suit', 'Filter', 'None').

detail.description: Quantitative atmosphere characteristics per WBH pp. 78-87. Fields are omitted when not applicable to the atmosphere code.

subtype_code.description: Atmosphere subtype code from the Exotic or Corrosive/Insidious Atmosphere Subtype table (WBH pp.85-86). Present only for codes A (10), B (11), C (12).

subtype_name.description: Atmosphere subtype name, e.g. 'Standard, Irritant' or 'Extremely Dense, Temperature 500K+'.

pressure_bar.description: Total atmospheric pressure at mean baseline altitude, in bar (WBH p.79). Omitted when pressure is unbound (subtype C/D/E).

oxygen_partial_pressure_bar.description: Partial pressure of oxygen at mean baseline altitude, in bar (WBH p.80). Only present for nitrogen-oxygen atmospheres.

scale_height_km.description: Atmospheric scale height in km, approximated as 8.5/gravity (WBH p.81).

taints.description: Atmosphere taints (WBH pp.82-85). Present only for tainted atmosphere codes (2, 4, 7, 9).

hazards.description: Insidious atmosphere hazards (WBH p.87). Present only for insidious (code C/12) atmospheres.

gases.description: Randomly selected hazardous gas components. Present only for Gas Mix (G) hazards.

gas_mix.description: Rolled gas components for Exotic/Corrosive/Insidious atmospheres (WBH pp.95+). Present only for codes 10/11/12.

min_safe_altitude_km.description: Code 13 (D): km above mean baseline (positive) where atmosphere becomes breathable. Code 14 (E): km below mean baseline (negative) required for breathability. Absent when no_safe_altitude is true or not applicable.

no_safe_altitude.description: True when no breathable altitude exists on this world (code 13: O2 too thin at N2-safe altitude; code 14: N2 narcosis at O2-safe depth).

unusual_subtypes.description: Rolled subtypes for Unusual (F) atmospheres (WBH pp.92-93). Present only for code 15.

profile.description: WBH p.82 atmosphere profile string in 'A-bar-ppo' form (e.g. '6-1.013-0.212' for Terra).

temperature.description: World temperature band, derived from a 2D roll modified by the atmosphere code.

description.description: Human-readable description of surface liquid coverage.

detail.description: Precise hydrographic characteristics. Absent for size-0 (belt) worlds.

surface_liquid_pct.description: Precise surface liquid coverage to the nearest 1%, drawn from a flat random distribution over the code's defined range (WBH p.93).

fluid_type.description: Primary fluid type covering the surface, determined by temperature zone (WBH pp.91-92). Absent for desert worlds (hydrographics 0) and gas-atmosphere worlds.

code.description: Population code. The population magnitude is roughly 10^code (e.g. 6 = millions).
Government code (0 = None/Anarchy; 4 = Representative Democracy; 13 = Religious Dictatorship; etc.).

law_level.description: Law Level (0 = no restrictions; higher values ban progressively more weapons and activities). No upper bound is enforced — high-government worlds can produce very high values.

tech_level.description: Technology Level (0 = Stone Age; 8 = present-day Earth; 15 = bleeding edge of Charted Space technology).

has_gas_giant.description: Whether the system contains at least one gas giant, enabling fuel skimming.

bases.description: Sorted list of base codes present in the system. Each letter represents one base type.

items.description: Base type code: C = Corsair, H = Highport, M = Military, N = Naval, S = Scout.

trade_codes.description: Sorted list of trade classification codes assigned to the world based on its characteristics.

items.description: Trade code: Ag=Agricultural, As=Asteroid, Ba=Barren, De=Desert, Fl=Fluid Oceans, Ga=Garden, Hi=High Population, Ht=High Tech, Ic=Ice-Capped, In=Industrial, Lo=Low Population, Lt=Low Tech, Na=Non-Agricultural, Ni=Non-Industrial, Po=Poor, Ri=Rich, Va=Vacuum, Wa=Waterworld.

travel_zone.description: Travel advisory zone. Green = safe; Amber = caution advised (hazardous environment or unstable government); Red = interdicted (referee-assigned only).

notes.description: Referee notes generated during world creation, such as warnings about Tech Level being below the minimum needed to maintain the atmosphere.
examples: TL 2 is below the minimum TL 8 needed to maintain Atmosphere 0 (None). Population may be doomed.

population_multiplier.description: Population multiplier (P digit) from the World Builder's Handbook. Refines the population code to one significant figure: a Population 6 world with P=3 has approximately 3,000,000 inhabitants. Range 1-9 for inhabited worlds; 0 for uninhabited worlds (Population 0).

belt_count.description: Number of planetoid belts in the system, from the World Builder's Handbook. A Size 0 mainworld (asteroid) contributes +1 to this count via the continuation method. Minimum 0.

gas_giant_count.description: Number of gas giants in the system. 0 if no gas giants are present. Generated from the World Builder's Handbook Gas Giant Quantity table when has_gas_giant is true; always 0 when has_gas_giant is false.

pbg.description: PBG string encoding Population multiplier, Belt count, and Gas giant count as a three-character string (e.g. '312' = P3, 1 belt, 2 gas giants). Each digit uses standard Traveller notation. Format introduced in MegaTraveller and used on Travellermap.com.

seed.description: The RNG seed used to generate this world. Pass this value as the 'seed' parameter to reproduce identical output. For system-endpoint responses, also pass the three boolean flags defined in $defs/system_generation_options.

population_detail.description: WBH Social Characteristics population profile. Present only when population detail generation has been explicitly requested. Includes PCR, urbanisation %, urban population, and major city populations.

p_value.description: Population multiplier digit (1–9), same as population_multiplier.

pcr.description: Population Concentration Rating (0 = extremely dispersed, 9 = extremely concentrated).

urbanisation_pct.description: Percentage of the world population living in urban settlements.

major_city_total_population.description: Combined population of all major cities.

cities.description: Individual major city entries, up to 10, sorted largest first.

codes.description: City designation codes, e.g. ['Cw'] for world capital.

population_profile.description: Compressed population profile string: P-p-C-%-M (pop code, P value, PCR, urbanisation %, major city count). Example: '6-5-3-39-7'.

size_detail.description: Physical characteristics of the mainworld. Present only when physical detail has been generated. Exactly one of two shapes: WorldPhysical (terrestrial) or BeltPhysical (asteroid belt).

oneOf.title: WorldPhysical
oneOf.description: Physical characteristics of a terrestrial mainworld (WBH pp. 74–77, 103–107).

axial_tilt_deg.description: Axial tilt in degrees. Values above 90° indicate retrograde rotation.

day_length_hours.description: Rotation period in hours (post-tidal final value).

basic_day_length_hours.description: Pre-tidal basic rotation period in hours, before tidal lock braking is applied.

tidal_status.description: Tidal lock status with the primary star (WBH pp. 105–107).

eccentricity_adjusted.description: Re-rolled eccentricity for 1:1 tidally-locked worlds with orbit_eccentricity > 0.1 (WBH p.77 Rule 4). Value is min(original_eccentricity, new_roll_with_DM-2). Absent when not applicable.

mean_temperature_k.description: Basic Mean Temperature in Kelvin (WBH p.47). Computed from orbital HZ deviation and atmosphere DMs. Present only when hz_deviation is available at generation time.

residual_seismic_stress.description: Residual Seismic Stress (WBH ~p.125). floor(Size - Age_Gyr + DMs)², treating values < 1 as 0 before squaring. Present after apply_moon_tidal_effects() is called.

tidal_seismic_stress.description: Tidal Seismic Stress (WBH ~p.127). Derived from primary mass, world size, eccentricity, orbital distance, and period. Present after apply_moon_tidal_effects() is called. Only emitted when > 0.

tidal_stress_factor.description: Tidal Stress Factor (WBH p.126): floor(tidal_amplitude_m / 10). Represents the seismic stress contribution from surface tidal amplitude (star + moons). Present after apply_moon_tidal_effects() is called. Only emitted when > 0.

total_seismic_stress.description: Total Seismic Stress = Residual Seismic Stress + Tidal Seismic Stress + Tidal Stress Factor. Present after apply_moon_tidal_effects() is called.

seismic_temperature_k.description: Mean temperature adjusted for seismic stress (WBH ~p.128): fourth_root(T^4 + TSS^4). Only present when total_seismic_stress > 0 and mean_temperature_k is available, and the adjustment changes the rounded value.

tidal_amplitude_m.description: Total surface tidal amplitude in metres (WBH pp.107-108): sum of star tidal effect and each qualifying moon's tidal effect on the planet. Star effect = (star_mass_solar × size) / (32 × AU³); moon effect = (moon_mass_earth × size) / (3.2 × (orbit_km / 1e6)³). Present when orbital data is available.

albedo.description: Rolled surface albedo (WBH pp.47-48). Present only when generate_advanced_mean_temperature() has been called.

greenhouse_factor.description: Rolled greenhouse factor G (WBH p.48). Present only when generate_advanced_mean_temperature() has been called.

advanced_mean_temperature_k.description: Physics-based mean temperature in Kelvin (WBH pp.47-48): 279 × ⁴√(L × (1−A) × (1+G) / AU²). Present only when generate_advanced_mean_temperature() has been called.

high_temperature_k.description: Physics-based high temperature bound in Kelvin (WBH pp.48-50, Steps 1-9): uses high luminosity (L × (1 + luminosity_modifier)) and near AU (AU × (1 − eccentricity)). Present only when generate_advanced_mean_temperature() has been called.

low_temperature_k.description: Physics-based low temperature bound in Kelvin (WBH pp.48-50, Steps 1-9): uses low luminosity (L × (1 − luminosity_modifier)) and far AU (AU × (1 + eccentricity)). Present only when generate_advanced_mean_temperature() has been called.

runaway_greenhouse.description: True when the optional runaway greenhouse check (WBH p.79) determined a runaway occurred. Absent when the check was not performed or did not trigger.

resource_rating.description: Terrestrial resource rating (WBH p.131): 2D-7 + Size + density DM, clamped to [2, 12]. Biological DMs (biomass, biodiversity, compatibility) are applied deterministically by attach_detail(). Present whenever generate_world_physical() has been called for a Size 1+ world.

resource_factor.description: Terrestrial resource factor (WBH p.131): resource_rating after depletion DM for Industrial/Agricultural worlds (subtract 1D-1, min 2) and TL8+ bonus (+1 per belt or gas giant in the system). Set by attach_detail().

oneOf.title: BeltPhysical
oneOf.description: Physical characteristics of an asteroid belt mainworld (WBH pp. 131–133).

inner_au.description: Inner boundary of belt span in AU.

outer_au.description: Outer boundary of belt span in AU.

other_pct.description: Residual 'other' percentage. m_type_pct + s_type_pct + c_type_pct + other_pct == 100.

size_s_bodies.description: Count of Size S significant planetoids.

mean_temperature_k.description: Basic Mean Temperature in K (WBH p.47). Computed with atmosphere DM 0.

biomass_rating.description: Native life biomass rating (WBH pp.127-131). 0 means no native life. Absent when not yet generated.

biocomplexity_rating.description: Biocomplexity rating (WBH pp.127-131). Absent when biomass is 0 or not yet generated.

native_sophont.description: True when a native sophont currently exists (WBH p.131). Absent when false.

extinct_sophont.description: True when evidence of an extinct native sophont exists (WBH p.131). Absent when false.

biodiversity_rating.description: Biodiversity rating (WBH p.130). Absent when biomass is 0 or not yet generated.

compatibility_rating.description: Compatibility rating (WBH p.130). Absent when biomass is 0 or not yet generated.

lifeform_profile.description: Four-character eHex lifeform profile MXDC (WBH p.130). Absent when biomass is 0 or not yet generated.

habitability_rating.description: Habitability rating (WBH p.131): 10 + DMs for size, atmosphere, hydrographics, tidal lock, temperature, and gravity. 0 = actively hostile; 10 = Terra-equivalent baseline. Absent when physical detail has not been generated.

tech_detail.description: WBH Social Characteristics tech level profile (§5). Present only when social detail generation has been explicitly requested. Includes high/low common TL, quality-of-life, transportation, and military sub-TLs, and a technology profile string.

tl_high_common.description: High common TL = UWP TL. The highest technology generally available.

tl_low_common.description: Low common TL. Floor of current technology in use; bounded [TL÷2, TL].

tl_air.description: Air transportation sub-TL. 0 when atmosphere = 0 and overall TL ≤ 5.

tl_military_personal.description: Personal military equipment sub-TL. 0 when law level = 0.

tl_novelty.description: Novelty TL (issue #154). Placeholder: equals tl_high_common until the WBH §5 per-nation procedure is implemented.

technology_profile.description: WBH technology profile string in format H-L-QQQQQ-TTTT-MM-N (all eHex digits). Example: 'C-8-BBA98-9870-A8-C'. The trailing -N segment is the Novelty TL (placeholder).

culture_detail.description: WBH Social Characteristics culture profile. Present only when culture detail generation has been explicitly requested. Includes 8 cultural traits (Diversity, Xenophilia, Uniqueness, Symbology, Cohesion, Progressiveness, Expansionism, Militancy) with labels and a compressed DXUS-CPEM profile string.

diversity.description: Diversity trait value (1–35). Indicates cultural monolithic vs. balkanised range.

diversity_label.description: Human-readable diversity label based on trait value.

xenophilia.description: Xenophilia trait value (1–35). Indicates openness to aliens (xenophobia to welcoming).

xenophilia_label.description: Human-readable xenophilia label based on trait value.

uniqueness.description: Uniqueness trait value (1–35). Indicates cultural distinctiveness.

uniqueness_label.description: Human-readable uniqueness label based on trait value.

symbology.description: Symbology trait value (1–35). Indicates extent of abstract symbolic meaning in culture.

symbology_label.description: Human-readable symbology label based on trait value.

cohesion.description: Cohesion trait value (1–35). Indicates individualism vs. collectivism.

cohesion_label.description: Human-readable cohesion label based on trait value.

progressiveness.description: Progressiveness trait value (1–35). Indicates cultural decay vs. innovation.

progressiveness_label.description: Human-readable progressiveness label based on trait value.

expansionism.description: Expansionism trait value (1–35). Indicates cultural insularity vs. imperialist expansion.

expansionism_label.description: Human-readable expansionism label based on trait value.

militancy.description: Militancy trait value (1–35). Indicates cultural peacefulness vs. militarism.

militancy_label.description: Human-readable militancy label based on trait value.

cultural_profile.description: Compressed cultural profile string in DXUS-CPEM format (9 chars: 4 eHex, hyphen, 4 eHex). D=Diversity, X=Xenophilia, U=Uniqueness, S=Symbology, C=Cohesion, P=Progressiveness, E=Expansionism, M=Militancy. Example: '7567-8432'.

importance_detail.description: CRB world importance score. Deterministic sum of modifiers from starport, population, tech level, trade codes, and base presence. Present only when social detail generation has been explicitly requested.

starport_dm.description: +1 for starport A or B; −1 for D, E, or X; 0 otherwise.

tech_dm.description: −1 for TL 0–8; 0 for TL 9; +1 for TL A–F (10–15); +2 for TL G+ (16+).

agricultural_dm.description: +1 if the Agricultural (Ag) trade code is present.

industrial_dm.description: +1 if the Industrial (In) trade code is present.

rich_dm.description: +1 if the Rich (Ri) trade code is present.

base_dm.description: +1 if two or more non-Corsair bases are present.

waystation_dm.description: +1 if an X-Boat waystation (base code W) is present.

labour_factor.description: Population code − 1, minimum 0. Represents the fraction of the population that directly contributes to wealth creation (WBH). 0 when population code is 0 or 1.

infrastructure_factor.description: Importance + population DMs (1D for pop 4–6; 2D for pop 7+). Absent when population is 0 or when the raw value is negative (no infrastructure). Present only when non-negative.

efficiency_factor.description: World efficiency factor (WBH p.131). Range -5 to +5; 0 is always treated as +1 (so the value is never 0). Absent until attach_importance_detail() runs. Government, law level, PCR, progressiveness, and expansionism all contribute DMs.

world_array.title: Traveller Mainworld Array
world_array.description: An array of mainworld records, as produced when --count > 1 is passed to traveller_world_gen.py.

system_generation_options.title: System Generation Options
system_generation_options.description: Boolean flags emitted at the TravellerSystem level (alongside 'seed') by all system endpoints. Together with 'seed' they uniquely identify the inputs needed to reproduce a system: pass seed + these three flags back to any system endpoint to obtain byte-identical output.

nhz_atmospheres.description: When true, worlds outside the habitable zone use WBH Non-Habitable Zone atmosphere tables (WBH pp.42-48). Pass ?nhz_atmospheres=true to reproduce. Default false.

orbital_eccentricity.description: When true, orbital eccentricity is rolled for all orbiting bodies (WBH p.27). Pass ?orbital_eccentricity=true to reproduce. Default false.

orbital_inclination.description: When true, orbital inclination is rolled for all orbiting bodies (WBH p.28). Pass ?orbital_inclination=true to reproduce. Default false.
